pain_point_label: Late or Non-Identification of At-Risk Learners

matches.match_rationale: Multi-site education operators hold structured student data — attendance records, LMS login frequency, assignment submission rates, grade book entries — that can feed a classifier trained to label students as at-risk vs. on-track. This directly addresses the pain mechanism: the classifier runs continuously, not just at exam time, so flagging happens early in the semester.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An early-warning dashboard ingests weekly structured signals (attendance, submission rate, grade trends) and classifies each student with an at-risk label and confidence tier, surfacing the list to their teacher or pastoral care coordinator.

matches.match_rationale: Propensity modeling predicts the probability that a specific student will fail or disengage before it happens, using behavioural features that accumulate across the semester. This is the closest capability to an early-warning system: it scores each individual and allows tiered intervention prioritisation rather than binary pass/fail at exam time.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A per-student propensity score — updated weekly from LMS and SIS data — feeds a ranked intervention queue for student support staff, with configurable thresholds per campus or year level.

matches.match_rationale: Academic failure is an adverse outcome with downstream consequences — dropout, government funding loss, reputational damage — making risk scoring a natural fit. A risk score stratifies the student cohort by severity of risk, which maps directly to how pastoral or learning-support teams triage their caseload across multiple campuses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A risk-stratified student cohort report, generated each fortnight, shows high/medium/low risk bands per campus and year level, enabling coordinated cross-site intervention resource allocation.

matches.match_rationale: The 'invisible student' sub-problem — quiet, non-failing students who are silently disengaging — is precisely an anomaly detection problem. Normal engagement patterns can be baselined; students whose behaviour deviates (sudden LMS drop-off, abrupt silence in participation metrics) are flagged as anomalous before grades reflect the problem.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An anomaly detector monitors per-student engagement time series and raises alerts when a student's behaviour diverges from their own historical baseline or peer cohort, targeting the students least likely to self-refer.

matches.match_rationale: A conversational agent embedded in the LMS or student portal can proactively check in with students — asking short diagnostic questions about confidence, workload stress, or comprehension — generating qualitative signals that complement quantitative grade/attendance data for students who are silent in class.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A weekly 2-minute check-in bot messages each student; low-confidence or distress responses trigger an alert to the relevant teacher, surfacing at-risk students who produce no other detectable signals.
